content.question: What is the enrollment threshold below which a village primary school becomes operationally or fiscally unsustainable? Does it vary by settlement geography, teacher-labor availability, or regional consolidation infrastructure?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a systematic census of all primary schools in depopulating Hungarian villages with current enrollment (6–14 age cohort), teacher staffing levels, and annual per-pupil expenditure, then map closure events or staffing reductions against enrollment size to estimate a cost-of-service viability boundary.
